acceptedQuestions.subject: Environment
acceptedQuestions.prompt: Which one of the following best describes the primary objective of the River Cities Alliance (RCA)?

options.text: To facilitate the interlinking of peninsular rivers to resolve inter-state water disputes.
To provide a dedicated platform for Indian cities to collaborate on river-sensitive urban planning.
To mandate the relocation of highly polluting industries away from the banks of major rivers.
To establish a statutory framework for penalizing municipalities that discharge untreated sewage into rivers.

acceptedQuestions.explanation: The River Cities Alliance (RCA) is an institutional framework designed to strengthen institutional capacity, foster inter-city collaboration, and support Urban River Management Plans (URMPs) for river-sensitive urban planning. It acts as a collaborative platform rather than a statutory penalizing body, and it is not related to river interlinking or mandating industrial relocation.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: The RCA focuses on capacity building and collaborative urban planning for sustainable river management, distinguishing it from statutory enforcement bodies or hard-infrastructure projects.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Objective: Strengthen institutional capacity, inter-city collaboration, and support Urban River Management Plans (URMPs). Institutional framework and initiatives for river-sensitive urban planning in Indian cities.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Excellent conceptual question. The distractors are highly plausible and represent common environmental or water-related initiatives, forcing the candidate to know the specific mandate of the RCA.

acceptedQuestions.subject: Environment
acceptedQuestions.prompt:
With reference to the River Cities Alliance (RCA), consider the following statements:

1. It is a joint initiative of the Ministry of Jal Shakti and the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
2. Membership in the alliance is open, allowing any river city to join at any time.
3. It aims to strengthen institutional capacity and support the preparation of Urban River Management Plans (URMPs).

How many of the above statements are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation:
Statement 1 is incorrect: The RCA is a joint initiative of the Ministry of Jal Shakti and the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, not the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Statement 2 is correct: The alliance currently has 145 member cities, and any river city can join at any time.
Statement 3 is correct: Its objective is to strengthen institutional capacity, inter-city collaboration, and support Urban River Management Plans (URMPs).
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: The River Cities Alliance is a joint initiative of MoJS and MoHUA launched in 2021. It is open to all river cities and aims to support Urban River Management Plans.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Joint initiative of Ministry of Jal Shakti and Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, launched in 2021. Objective: Strengthen institutional capacity, inter-city collaboration, and support Urban River Management Plans (URMPs)... Currently has 145 member cities, and any river city can join at any time.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Good statement-based question testing the nodal ministries and core features of the RCA. Distractors are plausible.

reviewerNotes: Selected two strong questions: one statement-based testing factual details (ministries, membership, URMPs) and one conceptual testing the core objective of the RCA. Both align well with UPSC Prelims standards.
